title: ~~The org's Keycloak instance will be accessible to ACMP from the deployment VM at all times; ACMP does not need to manage a separate IdP.~~ **RESOLVED — FALSE (ADR-0015):** the org has no Keycloak. M
statement: ~~The org's Keycloak instance will be accessible to ACMP from the deployment VM at all times; ACMP does not need to manage a separate IdP.~~ **RESOLVED — FALSE (ADR-0015):** the org has no Keycloak. Mitigation realized: Keycloak is now provisioned as a bundled service in the ACMP Compose stack with an ACMP-owned realm (the "scope expansion" path was taken).
risk_if_wrong: If false: ACMP needs an internal fallback login path or Keycloak must be provisioned as part of the Compose stack — a scope expansion. **This is now the adopted design (ADR-0015).**
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-001", "Assumption": "~~The org's Keycloak instance will be accessible to ACMP from the deployment VM at all times; ACMP does not need to manage a separate IdP.~~ **RESOLVED — FALSE (ADR-0015):** the org has no Keycloak. Mitigation realized: Keycloak is now provisioned as a bundled service in the ACMP Compose stack with an ACMP-owned realm (the \"scope expansion\" path was taken).", "Basis": "Rested on the org already running a reachable Keycloak IdP that ACMP could consume; resolved FALSE per ADR-0015 — the org has no Keycloak, so Keycloak is now bundled in the ACMP Compose stack with an ACMP-owned realm.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: ACMP needs an internal fallback login path or Keycloak must be provisioned as part of the Compose stack — a scope expansion. **This is now the adopted design (ADR-0015).**", "Status": "Approved", "Owner": "Tech Lead"}}

title: Keycloak group/realm-role claims can be configured to carry ACMP's canonical role names (or a mapping can be agreed) without code change to Keycloak itself.
statement: Keycloak group/realm-role claims can be configured to carry ACMP's canonical role names (or a mapping can be agreed) without code change to Keycloak itself.
risk_if_wrong: If false: ACMP must support a custom claim-mapping configuration layer (already planned in BL-019) or Keycloak admin deploys a custom OIDC mapper.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-002", "Assumption": "Keycloak group/realm-role claims can be configured to carry ACMP's canonical role names (or a mapping can be agreed) without code change to Keycloak itself.", "Basis": "Keycloak realm-level claim mapping is configurable, so canonical ACMP role names can be emitted or mapped without modifying Keycloak's own code.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: ACMP must support a custom claim-mapping configuration layer (already planned in BL-019) or Keycloak admin deploys a custom OIDC mapper.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Tech Lead / Keycloak Admin"}}

title: All committee users (≤20) have access to the deployment VM's network (internal network or VPN); no public internet exposure required.
statement: All committee users (≤20) have access to the deployment VM's network (internal network or VPN); no public internet exposure required.
risk_if_wrong: If false: TLS termination + network exposure policy must be designed and approved, widening the security perimeter review.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-003", "Assumption": "All committee users (≤20) have access to the deployment VM's network (internal network or VPN); no public internet exposure required.", "Basis": "All ≤20 committee users sit on the internal network or VPN, so ACMP needs no public internet exposure.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: TLS termination + network exposure policy must be designed and approved, widening the security perimeter review.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Secretary"}}

title: SQL Server will be provisioned on the same network segment as the ACMP Docker Compose stack, with a connection string accessible via environment variable. ACMP owns its own SQL Server instance and sch
statement: SQL Server will be provisioned on the same network segment as the ACMP Docker Compose stack, with a connection string accessible via environment variable. ACMP owns its own SQL Server instance and schema.
risk_if_wrong: If false: shared SQL Server requires schema isolation and resource governance configuration, adding operational risk.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-004", "Assumption": "SQL Server will be provisioned on the same network segment as the ACMP Docker Compose stack, with a connection string accessible via environment variable. ACMP owns its own SQL Server instance and schema.", "Basis": "ACMP owns a dedicated SQL Server instance/schema on the same network segment as the Compose stack, reachable via an environment-variable connection string.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: shared SQL Server requires schema isolation and resource governance configuration, adding operational risk.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Tech Lead"}}

title: MinIO can be provisioned as a Docker container in the ACMP Compose stack (or as a separately provisioned S3-compatible endpoint) with adequate storage volume mounted from the host.
statement: MinIO can be provisioned as a Docker container in the ACMP Compose stack (or as a separately provisioned S3-compatible endpoint) with adequate storage volume mounted from the host.
risk_if_wrong: If false: a different S3-compatible provider must be configured behind `IFileStore`; no code change required (abstraction layer), but ops setup changes.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-005", "Assumption": "MinIO can be provisioned as a Docker container in the ACMP Compose stack (or as a separately provisioned S3-compatible endpoint) with adequate storage volume mounted from the host.", "Basis": "MinIO (or another S3-compatible endpoint) can run as a Compose container behind `IFileStore` with host-mounted storage, so no external object store is required.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: a different S3-compatible provider must be configured behind `IFileStore`; no code change required (abstraction layer), but ops setup changes.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Tech Lead"}}

title: The committee operates a single committee with a stable, known membership; the canonical roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, etc.) map cleanly to real people. There will be no need to model sub-commit
statement: The committee operates a single committee with a stable, known membership; the canonical roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, etc.) map cleanly to real people. There will be no need to model sub-committees or working groups in v1.
risk_if_wrong: If false: multi-committee model is a significant scope expansion (OOS, explicitly excluded by settled decision).
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-006", "Assumption": "The committee operates a single committee with a stable, known membership; the canonical roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, etc.) map cleanly to real people. There will be no need to model sub-committees or working groups in v1.", "Basis": "The organization runs a single committee with stable, known membership mapping cleanly to canonical roles; no sub-committees or working groups are modelled in v1.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: multi-committee model is a significant scope expansion (OOS, explicitly excluded by settled decision).", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Secretary / Chairman"}}

title: Dates are Gregorian only; no Hijri calendar support is required in v1 (settled decision, README §A).
statement: Dates are Gregorian only; no Hijri calendar support is required in v1 (settled decision, README §A).
risk_if_wrong: If false: Hijri calendar is a Phase 3+ item requiring a calendar library and locale configuration that is not currently planned.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-007", "Assumption": "Dates are Gregorian only; no Hijri calendar support is required in v1 (settled decision, README §A).", "Basis": "Gregorian-only dating is a settled decision (README §A); Hijri is not required in v1.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: Hijri calendar is a Phase 3+ item requiring a calendar library and locale configuration that is not currently planned.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Secretary"}}

title: The platform will be used by ≤20 total users; no horizontal scaling, no HA cluster, no heavy performance engineering is required. 99.9% availability is achievable via a simple standby VM + nightly bac
statement: The platform will be used by ≤20 total users; no horizontal scaling, no HA cluster, no heavy performance engineering is required. 99.9% availability is achievable via a simple standby VM + nightly backup (settled, README §A).
risk_if_wrong: If false: a load balancer, multiple app instances, and session-state externalization are needed — an architectural change.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-008", "Assumption": "The platform will be used by ≤20 total users; no horizontal scaling, no HA cluster, no heavy performance engineering is required. 99.9% availability is achievable via a simple standby VM + nightly backup (settled, README §A).", "Basis": "The ≤20-user scale ceiling is settled (README §A), so 99.9% availability is met by a standby VM + nightly backup without HA or scaling engineering.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: a load balancer, multiple app instances, and session-state externalization are needed — an architectural change.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Tech Lead"}}

title: The organization does not require a public-facing portal or external partner access in v1; ACMP is an internal-only tool.
statement: The organization does not require a public-facing portal or external partner access in v1; ACMP is an internal-only tool.
risk_if_wrong: If false: a separate security-reviewed external-access layer is required (OOS-12).
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-009", "Assumption": "The organization does not require a public-facing portal or external partner access in v1; ACMP is an internal-only tool.", "Basis": "ACMP is an internal-only tool in v1; no public portal or external partner access is required.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: a separate security-reviewed external-access layer is required (OOS-12).", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Chairman"}}

title: The Secretary is the primary product owner and has authority to make functional scope decisions during delivery without escalating each to a governance board.
statement: The Secretary is the primary product owner and has authority to make functional scope decisions during delivery without escalating each to a governance board.
risk_if_wrong: If false: decision latency will slow sprints; a lightweight approval process must be defined.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-010", "Assumption": "The Secretary is the primary product owner and has authority to make functional scope decisions during delivery without escalating each to a governance board.", "Basis": "The Secretary is the primary product owner with authority to make functional scope decisions during delivery without per-decision governance-board escalation.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: decision latency will slow sprints; a lightweight approval process must be defined.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Secretary"}}

title: The urgency SLA thresholds (Critical: 3 days in status, Urgent: 7 days, Normal: 21 days) are acceptable starting values; the committee will adjust them via configuration after PH-1 pilot. [Marked `[un
statement: The urgency SLA thresholds (Critical: 3 days in status, Urgent: 7 days, Normal: 21 days) are acceptable starting values; the committee will adjust them via configuration after PH-1 pilot. [Marked `[unverified]` in FR-038; must be validated with committee.]
risk_if_wrong: If false: SLA thresholds must be redesigned before PH-1 go-live; aging indicator calibration changes.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-011", "Assumption": "The urgency SLA thresholds (Critical: 3 days in status, Urgent: 7 days, Normal: 21 days) are acceptable starting values; the committee will adjust them via configuration after PH-1 pilot. [Marked `[unverified]` in FR-038; must be validated with committee.]", "Basis": "The urgency SLA thresholds (Critical 3d / Urgent 7d / Normal 21d) are proposed as acceptable starting values to be tuned via configuration after the PH-1 pilot; marked `[unverified]` in FR-038 pending committee validation.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: SLA thresholds must be redesigned before PH-1 go-live; aging indicator calibration changes.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Secretary"}}

title: The committee is willing to adopt the canonical decision outcome vocabulary (11 outcomes in README §E) as-is, possibly with bilingual labels; no additional custom outcomes are required.
statement: The committee is willing to adopt the canonical decision outcome vocabulary (11 outcomes in README §E) as-is, possibly with bilingual labels; no additional custom outcomes are required.
risk_if_wrong: If false: the outcome enum is expanded; no code architecture change but schema + UI changes required.

title: Webex is the only external meeting/collaboration platform in use by the committee; no Teams or Zoom integration is needed.
statement: Webex is the only external meeting/collaboration platform in use by the committee; no Teams or Zoom integration is needed.
risk_if_wrong: If false: a second meeting-platform adapter must be designed, widening Phase 2 scope.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-013", "Assumption": "Webex is the only external meeting/collaboration platform in use by the committee; no Teams or Zoom integration is needed.", "Basis": "Webex is the committee's only external meeting/collaboration platform; no Teams or Zoom integration is needed.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: a second meeting-platform adapter must be designed, widening Phase 2 scope.", "Status": "Proposed", "Owner": "Secretary"}}

title: The AC-029 downstream-link gate (P8d, OQ-045) proxies "≥1 downstream artifact" as **≥1 ActionItem sourced from the decision** (`SourceType=Decision, SourceId`), and **exempts the supersession successo
statement: The AC-029 downstream-link gate (P8d, OQ-045) proxies "≥1 downstream artifact" as **≥1 ActionItem sourced from the decision** (`SourceType=Decision, SourceId`), and **exempts the supersession successor** from the gate. Rationale: the Risk module + typed-edge Traceability (ADR-0008) land in P10, so Actions is the only linkable artifact today; and a supersession successor is drafted+issued atomically with zero links, so gating it would deadlock every correction (the successor corrects a prior that already passed the gate). `SourceId` is trusted input — no cross-module existence check.
risk_if_wrong: If false (e.g. a decision must link a Risk with no Action, or supersession must itself re-satisfy the gate): the gate predicate widens when P10 adds Risk/typed edges, and the supersession path would need its own link-carry-forward or a draft-then-issue split. No schema change; the `IActionLinkDirectory` predicate + handler check are the only touch-points.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-014", "Assumption": "The AC-029 downstream-link gate (P8d, OQ-045) proxies \"≥1 downstream artifact\" as **≥1 ActionItem sourced from the decision** (`SourceType=Decision, SourceId`), and **exempts the supersession successor** from the gate. Rationale: the Risk module + typed-edge Traceability (ADR-0008) land in P10, so Actions is the only linkable artifact today; and a supersession successor is drafted+issued atomically with zero links, so gating it would deadlock every correction (the successor corrects a prior that already passed the gate). `SourceId` is trusted input — no cross-module existence check.", "Basis": "Actions is the only linkable downstream artifact today (the Risk module + typed-edge Traceability land in P10), and a supersession successor is drafted+issued atomically with zero links, so gating it would deadlock every correction; `SourceId` is trusted input with no cross-module existence check.", "Risk if wrong": "If false (e.g. a decision must link a Risk with no Action, or supersession must itself re-satisfy the gate): the gate predicate widens when P10 adds Risk/typed edges, and the supersession path would need its own link-carry-forward or a draft-then-issue split. No schema change; the `IActionLinkDirectory` predicate + handler check are the only touch-points.", "Status": "Approved", "Owner": "Tech Lead / Architect"}}

title: **P10c Traceability backbone assumptions.** (1) *No physical Artifact registry* — relationship edges self-describe via value snapshots `(Type, Id, Key, Title)` on both ends; matches docs/domain/search
statement: **P10c Traceability backbone assumptions.** (1) *No physical Artifact registry* — relationship edges self-describe via value snapshots `(Type, Id, Key, Title)` on both ends; matches docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §2.1 SQL and reconciles ADR-0008's "registry" prescription via **ADR-0019** (amends 0008). (2) *AC-029 widened to a **curated** downstream set* — an edge satisfies the gate iff the decision is source of `recorded-as`/`resolves` or target of `implements`; upstream/lineage edges (`decided-by`/`derived-from`/`supersedes`) are excluded so a decision cannot be self-satisfied by its own topic. Superset of the Action-only gate → AC-029 stays Met. (3) *Title is a create-time snapshot* — may go stale on rename (accepted; deep-link fetches live data; keys never go stale). (4) *Owner-AiO edge-create deferred* — v1 create is Chairman/Secretary RBAC only (no ABAC handler for arbitrary artifact types; no UI). (5) *Panel shows explicit edges only* — projection of existing soft-refs (Decision→Topic, Action→source, Risk→subject) into edges is a later slice, and when built should be **read-time contract composition, not write-through** (one source of truth; keeps the upstream `decided-by` edge out of the AC-029 predicate). (6) *Edge endpoint types are NOT validated against the docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §2.2 per-RelType matrix* — the create validator checks `IsInEnum` + source≠target only, not that (e.g.) `RecordedAs` runs Decision→ADR. Trusted-actor input (Chairman/Secretary only, mirrors ASM-014's `SourceId` posture). **Consequence:** a malformed edge (e.g. `Decision --RecordedAs--> Meeting`) can satisfy the AC-029 gate, since `DecisionHasDownstreamEdgeAsync` matches on source-type + RelType without checking the far-endpoint type. Acceptable at ≤20 on-prem users; the full 16-RelType type matrix is deferred.
risk_if_wrong: If false: (1) a registry + per-module edge-writes would be needed (ADR-0001 cost); (2) the downstream RelType set is re-curated; (3) a read-time title-resolution seam is added; (4) an artifact-generic ABAC resource handler is built; (5) projection is added as its own slice; (6) the §2.2 per-RelType endpoint-type matrix is added to the validator. All are additive — no rework of the P10c edge schema or the Shared `ITraceabilityLinks` port.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-015", "Assumption": "**P10c Traceability backbone assumptions.** (1) *No physical Artifact registry* — relationship edges self-describe via value snapshots `(Type, Id, Key, Title)` on both ends; matches docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §2.1 SQL and reconciles ADR-0008's \"registry\" prescription via **ADR-0019** (amends 0008). (2) *AC-029 widened to a **curated** downstream set* — an edge satisfies the gate iff the decision is source of `recorded-as`/`resolves` or target of `implements`; upstream/lineage edges (`decided-by`/`derived-from`/`supersedes`) are excluded so a decision cannot be self-satisfied by its own topic. Superset of the Action-only gate → AC-029 stays Met. (3) *Title is a create-time snapshot* — may go stale on rename (accepted; deep-link fetches live data; keys never go stale). (4) *Owner-AiO edge-create deferred* — v1 create is Chairman/Secretary RBAC only (no ABAC handler for arbitrary artifact types; no UI). (5) *Panel shows explicit edges only* — projection of existing soft-refs (Decision→Topic, Action→source, Risk→subject) into edges is a later slice, and when built should be **read-time contract composition, not write-through** (one source of truth; keeps the upstream `decided-by` edge out of the AC-029 predicate). (6) *Edge endpoint types are NOT validated against the docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §2.2 per-RelType matrix* — the create validator checks `IsInEnum` + source≠target only, not that (e.g.) `RecordedAs` runs Decision→ADR. Trusted-actor input (Chairman/Secretary only, mirrors ASM-014's `SourceId` posture). **Consequence:** a malformed edge (e.g. `Decision --RecordedAs--> Meeting`) can satisfy the AC-029 gate, since `DecisionHasDownstreamEdgeAsync` matches on source-type + RelType without checking the far-endpoint type. Acceptable at ≤20 on-prem users; the full 16-RelType type matrix is deferred.", "Basis": "Relationship edges self-describe via value snapshots (no physical Artifact registry, per docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §2.1 and ADR-0019 amending ADR-0008); the AC-029 gate is widened to a curated downstream edge set (superset of the Action-only gate); titles are create-time snapshots; Owner-AiO create and soft-ref projection are deferred; endpoints are trusted-actor input not validated against the per-RelType matrix — all acceptable at ≤20 on-prem users.", "Risk if wrong": "If false: (1) a registry + per-module edge-writes would be needed (ADR-0001 cost); (2) the downstream RelType set is re-curated; (3) a read-time title-resolution seam is added; (4) an artifact-generic ABAC resource handler is built; (5) projection is added as its own slice; (6) the §2.2 per-RelType endpoint-type matrix is added to the validator. All are additive — no rework of the P10c edge schema or the Shared `ITraceabilityLinks` port.", "Status": "Approved", "Owner": "Tech Lead / Architect"}}

title: **P10d Dependencies backbone assumptions.** (1) *Read-time composition, NO write-through mirror edge (OQ-046)* — a `Dependency` is stored ONCE (Dependencies module); it is NOT mirrored into the Tracea
statement: **P10d Dependencies backbone assumptions.** (1) *Read-time composition, NO write-through mirror edge (OQ-046)* — a `Dependency` is stored ONCE (Dependencies module); it is NOT mirrored into the Traceability `Relationship` table. docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §1.34/§1.1 previously implied write-through sync; per-module DbContexts share no transaction, so a mirror would risk dual-write drift for no benefit. The unified graph composes at read-time via the Dependencies module's artifact-scoped query. **Consequence:** the docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §4.1 recursive-CTE impact traversal will NOT see dependency edges — **P10f must UNION the Dependencies table** (or compose in memory). Two-model boundary affirmed (docs/domain/domain-model.md §A.3; ADR-0008/0019). (2) *Kind = `{DependsOn,BlockedBy,Blocks,RelatesTo}`* — the design register's 4 chips; `Impacts` (docs/domain/domain-model.md) dropped as unused, `Blocks` added (FR-094 + design). (3) *`IsCrossStream` not modelled* — `(derived)` per docs/domain/domain-model.md, computed read-time in P10e from real stream *sets* (FR-095 needs a Topics+Membership stream-resolution contract, owned by P10e); P10d stores no flag. (4) *`Note` = plain `string?`* not `LocalizedString` (single free-text Notes box; user annotation ≠ system label; mirrors `Relationship.Notes`). (5) *Endpoint types not validated against a per-Kind matrix* (trusted actor, mirrors ASM-015 pt6). (6) *Member/Reviewer `Dependency.Create` AiO dormant* — the endpoint is resourceless so `CapabilityHandler` grants only Chairman/Secretary; AiO is deferred because **there is no FE caller yet**, NOT because auth cannot express it (a topic-sourced create satisfies the existing `ITopicCapabilityResolver` once P10e supplies `ITopicScopedResource`; only `action→action` is genuinely resolver-blocked). (7) *Resolve/Remove reuse `Policies.DependencyCreate`* (no `Dependency.Manage` policy — same trusted actors, YAGNI).
risk_if_wrong: If false: (1) a Dependencies→Traceability write port + outbox/reconciliation would be needed (ADR-0001 cost), or unify the two aggregates (reverses ADR-0008 — rejected, see progress-log P10d); (2) the Kind enum is re-curated; (3) a stream-resolution seam is built earlier; (4) a bilingual Note is modelled; (5) the per-Kind endpoint matrix is added; (6) an `ITopicScopedResource` is wired at the create endpoint (topic-sourced) + an artifact-generic ABAC handler (action-sourced); (7) a `Dependency.Manage` policy is split out. All additive — no rework of the P10d schema.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "ASM-016", "Assumption": "**P10d Dependencies backbone assumptions.** (1) *Read-time composition, NO write-through mirror edge (OQ-046)* — a `Dependency` is stored ONCE (Dependencies module); it is NOT mirrored into the Traceability `Relationship` table. docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §1.34/§1.1 previously implied write-through sync; per-module DbContexts share no transaction, so a mirror would risk dual-write drift for no benefit. The unified graph composes at read-time via the Dependencies module's artifact-scoped query. **Consequence:** the docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §4.1 recursive-CTE impact traversal will NOT see dependency edges — **P10f must UNION the Dependencies table** (or compose in memory). Two-model boundary affirmed (docs/domain/domain-model.md §A.3; ADR-0008/0019). (2) *Kind = `{DependsOn,BlockedBy,Blocks,RelatesTo}`* — the design register's 4 chips; `Impacts` (docs/domain/domain-model.md) dropped as unused, `Blocks` added (FR-094 + design). (3) *`IsCrossStream` not modelled* — `(derived)` per docs/domain/domain-model.md, computed read-time in P10e from real stream *sets* (FR-095 needs a Topics+Membership stream-resolution contract, owned by P10e); P10d stores no flag. (4) *`Note` = plain `string?`* not `LocalizedString` (single free-text Notes box; user annotation ≠ system label; mirrors `Relationship.Notes`). (5) *Endpoint types not validated against a per-Kind matrix* (trusted actor, mirrors ASM-015 pt6). (6) *Member/Reviewer `Dependency.Create` AiO dormant* — the endpoint is resourceless so `CapabilityHandler` grants only Chairman/Secretary; AiO is deferred because **there is no FE caller yet**, NOT because auth cannot express it (a topic-sourced create satisfies the existing `ITopicCapabilityResolver` once P10e supplies `ITopicScopedResource`; only `action→action` is genuinely resolver-blocked). (7) *Resolve/Remove reuse `Policies.DependencyCreate`* (no `Dependency.Manage` policy — same trusted actors, YAGNI).", "Basis": "Read-time composition with no write-through mirror edge (OQ-046) avoids dual-write drift across per-module DbContexts; the curated Kind enum, read-time `IsCrossStream` derivation, plain-string `Note`, trusted-actor endpoints, dormant Member/Reviewer AiO, and reused create policy are all right-sized for the two-model boundary (ADR-0008/0019).", "Risk if wrong": "If false: (1) a Dependencies→Traceability write port + outbox/reconciliation would be needed (ADR-0001 cost), or unify the two aggregates (reverses ADR-0008 — rejected, see progress-log P10d); (2) the Kind enum is re-curated; (3) a stream-resolution seam is built earlier; (4) a bilingual Note is modelled; (5) the per-Kind endpoint matrix is added; (6) an `ITopicScopedResource` is wired at the create endpoint (topic-sourced) + an artifact-generic ABAC handler (action-sourced); (7) a `Dependency.Manage` policy is split out. All additive — no rework of the P10d schema.", "Status": "Approved", "Owner": "Tech Lead / Architect"}}
